title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Hemorrhoids
intro: Herbal teas for Hemorrhoids are natural remedies that utilize the medicinal properties of various plants to alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids, a condition characterized by swollen veins in the rectal area. These teas can be an effective treatment option due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties, which help to reduce pain, itching, and swelling. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat hemorrhoids include witch hazel, which astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, chamomile, which soothes and calms the digestive system, and peppermint, which reduces inflammation and eases digestion. Other herbal teas that can be beneficial include dandelion root, which acts as a natural diuretic to reduce water retention, aloe vera, which has anti-inflammatory properties to soothe the rectal area, and ginger, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce pain and inflammation.

preparations.herb_desc: Hamamelis virginiana teas, also known as witch hazel, have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The tannins present in this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and prevent bleeding associated with hemorrhoids. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Hamamelis virginiana teas possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which contribute to their therapeutic effects in treating hemorrhoids. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Hamamelis virginiana teas offer a natural and non-invasive approach to managing hemorrhoid symptoms and promoting overall rectal health.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Use 2 tablespoons of the cleaned leaves and flowers. Chop them into small pieces.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Remove from heat.
Add the chopped leaves and flowers to the hot water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day to help with hemorrhoids.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hamamelis virginiana teas can be effective in treating hemorrhoids, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. To use Hamamelis virginiana teas safely, precautions include using it in moderation, avoiding it if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, and not consuming it if you are pregnant or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Hamamelis virginiana tea 2-3 times a day
Take 1-2 tablespoons of dried leaves as a supplement if needed
Consume for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable improvements
Monitor your condition and adjust dosage as necessary
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen after 4 weeks

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The mucilages present in Aloe barbadensis teas help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with hemorrhoids, while also promoting the healing of the affected area. The bioactive constituents, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in reducing pain and discomfort. By consuming Aloe barbadensis teas, individuals can benefit from reduced symptoms of hemorrhoids, including pain, itching, and bleeding, as well as improved overall digestive health.
preparation_procedure: Wash 2 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis gel with water. Make sure it's clean.
Add the cleaned Aloe gel to 1 cup of boiling water. Stir gently.
Let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes. Reduce heat to low.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed. Store the rest in the fridge.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Plastic and reconstructive surgery", Aloe barbadensis teas for hemorrhoids may not be directly supported by the given study as it focuses on the effect of aloe vera gel extract on burn wound healing rather than hemorrhoids, but since aloe vera has been found to decrease wound bacterial counts and aid faster healing, it may be beneficial in treating hemorrhoids by reducing bacterial infections and promoting healing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals due to its natural compounds and potential interactions with medications. When using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat hemorrhoids, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, avoiding consumption if you have stomach ulcers or are pregnant or breastfeeding, and monitoring your body's response to the herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Soak a cloth in cooled Aloe vera tea for 10 minutes.
Gently apply the soaked cloth to affected area for 30 minutes.
Use 1 cup of Aloe vera tea as sitz bath for 15-20 minutes, 3 times a day.
Drink 1 cup of Aloe vera tea 2 times a day for internal relief.
Continue using Aloe vera tea for 1 week to see noticeable improvement.

preparations.herb_desc: Plantago lanceolata teas have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory, astringent, and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing swelling and pain, promoting wound healing, and preventing bacterial infections. The bioactive constituents of Plantago lanceolata teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting platelet aggregation, reducing oxidative stress, and exerting antimicrobial activity. The benefits of using Plantago lanceolata teas to treat hemorrhoids include reduced discomfort, accelerated healing, and prevention of complications, making it a natural and effective alternative remedy.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers. Clean and dry them.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers. Place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water. Pour the boiling water over the dried Plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms. Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for later use.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Plantago lanceolata teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including monitoring for signs of excessive bleeding, avoiding use during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and being aware of potential interactions with other herbal remedies.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Plantago lanceolata tea for hemorrhoids.
Drink 1 cup of Plantago lanceolata tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
Continue using for 2-4 weeks, as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with this condition. The bioactive constituents, including quinine, quinidine, and cinchonine, present in these teas help to constrict blood vessels, reduce bleeding, and promote healing of the affected area. Regular consumption of Cinchona officinalis teas has been found to be beneficial in treating hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation, soothing irritated tissues, and improving overall digestive health. The antiseptic properties of these teas also help to prevent infection and promote a speedy recovery from hemorrhoids.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the bark.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce hemorrhoid symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly if consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To avoid potential complications, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and also to be cautious when combining Cinchona officinalis teas with other medications or herbal preparations that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis tea 2-3 times a day
Consume 1-2 teaspoons of Cinchona bark powder per cup of tea
Take Cinchona tea for 5-7 days to see noticeable improvement
Increase tea consumption to 3-4 cups a day if symptoms persist
Consult a doctor if symptoms worsen or do not improve after 7 days

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas, also known as horsetail tea, have been used traditionally to treat hemorrhoids due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The bioactive constituents, such as silica and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote the healing of tissue damage associated with hemorrhoids. The astringent properties of Equisetum arvense teas help to constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing swelling and bleeding, while also promoting the formation of scar tissue that can help to alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids. The benefits of using Equisetum arvense teas to treat hemorrhoids include reduced inflammation, alleviated pain, and improved overall health, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Equisetum arvense (Horsetail) roots or stems.
Combine the Horsetail with 4 cups of water in a pot.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the Horsetail. Let the tea cool.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with hemorrhoid relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues such as nausea and diarrhea, and interactions with other medications. Precautions to take when using Equisetum arvense teas to treat hemorrhoids include monitoring your body's response and reducing the dosage if you experience any adverse effects, as well as being cautious when consuming it with other medications or supplements.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Equisetum arvense tea 2 times a day.
Start with small dosages of 1/2 cup per serving.
Increase dosage to 1 cup if symptoms persist after 2 days.
Use Equisetum arvense tea for 2-4 weeks for optimal results.
Consult a healthcare professional for severe or persistent hemorrhoids.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as stinging nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, help to reduce inflammation, improve blood circulation, and constrict blood vessels, which alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids. Drinking Urtica dioica tea may help to reduce pain, itching, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids, making it a potential natural remedy for this condition. The benefits of using Urtica dioica tea to treat hemorrhoids include reduced discomfort, faster recovery, and minimal side effects, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural and holistic approach to managing this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Urtica dioica leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the leaves in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from hemorrhoids.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Life sciences", Urtica dioica teas may be commonly used in patients with mild-moderate symptoms of hemorrhoids, similar to its application in patients with mild-moderate symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used as a potential remedy for hemorrhoids, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, especially those with sensitive skin or allergies. Precautions when using Urtica dioica teas to treat hemorrhoids include avoiding excessive consumption, which may lead to interactions with other medications, and being cautious when applying the tea externally to sensitive areas, as it can cause irritation.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Urtica dioica tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 tablespoons of dried Urtica dioica leaves per cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain and drink the tea immediately, while still warm.
Continue for 1-2 weeks to help soothe and reduce hemorrhoids.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote healing in the affected area. These herbal preparations help to treat hemorrhoids by improving blood circulation, reducing swelling, and protecting the mucous membranes from further irritation. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat hemorrhoids include reduced pain and discomfort, faster recovery, and a decrease in the risk of complications such as bleeding or infection.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried root to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the root.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but using it may cause side effects such as water retention, high blood pressure, and an irregular heartbeat due to its licorice content. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include monitoring blood pressure, limiting consumption to short periods, and avoiding it altogether if you have a history of heart or kidney problems.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried roots for each 1 cup of water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes to allow active compounds to release.
Drink the tea before meals to ease digestion and reduce strain.
Continue for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable improvements in symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas, also known as cranberry tea, have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The anthocyanins and proanthocyanidins present in this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. By improving venous circulation and reducing swelling, Vaccinium macrocarpon teas can help to alleviate symptoms such as itching, pain, and bleeding. The benefits of using Vaccinium macrocarpon teas to treat hemorrhoids include reduced risk of infection and scarring, as well as a decrease in the need for surgical intervention.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Vaccinium macrocarpon berries, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser.
Rinse the berries and remove any stems or debris.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the water and heat it in a saucepan until the honey dissolves.
Add the Vaccinium macrocarpon berries to the tea infuser and steep in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 3 times a day to help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as diarrhea or stomach cramps, and allergic reactions like skin rashes or itching. Precautions should be taken when using Vaccinium macrocarpon teas for hemorrhoids, as excessive consumption may exacerbate bleeding, and pregnant or breastfeeding women should be cautious due to the potential effects on fetal or infant development.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Vaccinium macrocarpon tea 3 times a day.
Use 1-2 tablespoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes before straining the tea.
Drink the tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Althaea officinalis teas, derived from the root of the marshmallow plant, have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilages present in this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by forming a protective barrier on the irritated tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The bioactive constituents, including mucilages, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to the anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. The benefits of this herbal preparation include reduced swelling, improved healing, and alleviated symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for treating hemorrhoids.

Steep the Althaea officinalis root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, to help soothe hemorrhoid symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Althaea officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating hemorrhoids due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, and allergic reactions like skin rashes or itching. To use Althaea officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid using it for extended periods, and be cautious when consuming it with other medications that may interact with its active compounds, such as saponins and mucilages.

Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe hemorrhoid symptoms.

Avena sativa teas can be used to alleviate hemorrhoids symptoms, but its use may be associated with potential side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its saponin content, which can be irritating to the digestive system.

When using Avena sativa teas to treat hemorrhoids, it is essential to note that long-term consumption may lead to dependence on the herb, and it's also recommended to start with small doses to avoid an allergic reaction, such as skin rashes or itching.

The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids, as well as promoting the healing of damaged tissues. preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rumex crispus leaves and flowers. Rinse them with water.

Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes. Let it cool.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rumex crispus teas can be used as an herbal remedy to treat hemorrhoids, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications due to its potential to lower blood pressure and affect blood clotting. preparations.herb_desc: Aloe ferox teas have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with the condition. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-annoside that help to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and alleviate pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Aloe ferox teas help to treat hemorrhoids by providing relief from symptoms such as itching, burning, and bleeding. The benefits of using Aloe ferox teas to treat hemorrhoids include reduced risk of complications, faster recovery, and a natural approach to managing the condition without the side effects associated with pharmaceutical treatments.
preparation_procedure: Cut 2-3 Aloe ferox leaves from a healthy plant, making sure they are free of any damage or disease.
Remove the spines from the leaves using a knife or a spoon, being careful not to touch the gel inside.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and place them in a cup.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the cup with the Aloe ferox pieces. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe ferox teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those who are sensitive to aloe vera. Precautions include using the tea in moderation, monitoring its effect on the body, and avoiding use if experiencing any severe symptoms or allergic reactions, as well as not using it during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Rhamnus frangula teas, also known as buckthorn tea, have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The anthraquinones present in buckthorn tea, such as emodin and aloe-emodin, help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of hemorrhoidal tissues. By stimulating bowel movements and reducing constipation, buckthorn tea helps to alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids, including pain, itching, and swelling. Regular consumption of buckthorn tea has been found to be beneficial in treating hemorrhoids, as it helps to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of affected tissues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Rhamnus frangula leaves.
Use 1 cup of water to make a tea. Heat the water in a pot.
Add the Rhamnus frangula leaves to the hot water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool down.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from hemorrhoids symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Current pharmaceutical biotechnology, Rhamnus frangula teas for hemorrhoids may not be specifically mentioned, but the plant Cascara sagrada, also from the Rhamnaceae family, has laxative activity which could be beneficial for managing constipation that sometimes causes hemorrhoids.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rhamnus frangula teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions, which can be severe in some individuals. To use Rhamnus frangula teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, gradually increase as needed, and avoid using it for extended periods, as this may lead to dependence and worsen the condition.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and naphthodianthrones, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections associated with hemorrhoids. By soothing and calming the affected area, Hypericum perforatum teas can help to alleviate pain, itching, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, promoting faster healing and recovery. Regular consumption of Hypericum perforatum teas has been reported to provide relief from hemorrhoid symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for this common health issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture using a piece of cheesecloth or a tea infuser.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Hypericum perforatum tea, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, interactions with other medications, and increased risk of bleeding due to its blood-thinning properties. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include starting with low dosages to assess tolerance, avoiding excessive consumption, and being cautious of potential interactions with other medications or medical conditions, such as bleeding disorders or recent surgery.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas have been used to treat hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort. The bioactive constituents present in aloe vera teas, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from pain and inflammation associated with hemorrhoids. By consuming aloe vera teas, individuals can experience relief from symptoms such as itching, bleeding, and discomfort, promoting faster healing and recovery. The benefits of using aloe vera teas to treat hemorrhoids include reduced pain and inflammation, improved bowel movements, and a decreased risk of further complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe vera gel, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and 1/4 teaspoon of vitamin E oil to the Aloe vera gel.
Combine the Aloe vera gel mixture with the cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser and enjoy.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of integrative medicine, Aloe vera teas for hemorrhoids may be beneficial due to the presence of Aloe vera among the 6 most cited species in Iranian traditional medicine that have documented hemorrhoid-healing effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can be used to treat hemorrhoids, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs or allergies to aloe vera. To use aloe vera teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid taking it on an empty stomach, and to stop using it if you experience any severe or persistent side effects.
